question_text: A ball is dropped from a height. As it falls, what happens to its gravitational potential energy?

options.label: A
options.text: It stays the same throughout the fall

options.label: B
options.text: It decreases and is converted into kinetic energy

options.label: C
options.text: It increases as the ball falls faster

options.label: D
options.text: It is destroyed when the ball hits the ground

explanation: As a ball falls, it loses height so its gravitational potential energy decreases. This energy is transformed into kinetic energy (energy of movement), which increases as the ball speeds up. This is a direct application of conservation of energy—the energy doesn't disappear, it just changes form.
